sections.paragraph:
εὐδαιμονέω aor. inf. εὐδαιμονῆσαι (Just., A I, 3, 3 [Pla., Rep. 5, 473de]) a person who prospers or experiences some good fortune appears to be enjoying special favor from a divinity [δαίμων], hence be happy, fortunate (Trag., Hdt. et al.; Epict.; POxy 1593, 2; Philo; Jos., C. Ap. 1, 224; Just.) Dg 10:5.—Schmidt, Syn. IV 394–407, esp. 398–402. DELG s.v. δαίμων.

aor. aor. = aorist

inf. inf. = infinitive

Just Just , II a.d.—List 5

Pla Pla , V–IV b.c.; s. also Ps.-Plato—List 5

Trag.

Trag. = writer (s) of tragedy

trag. = tragedy, writer (s) of tragedy

Hdt. Hdt. = Herodotus, V b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

Epict Epict , various works, I–II a.d.—List 5

POxy POxy = Oxyrhynchus Papyri—List 4

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

Dg Dg = Letter of Diognetus, attributed to an unidentifiable apologist; II a.d.—List 1

Schmidt, Syn. Schmidt, Syn. = JS., Synonymik—List 6

esp. esp. = especially

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 404
